title: The Birdhouse Farm
story_html:
Sam lived in a small house at the edge of town. His class was going on a trip to Mr. Lee's farm to learn about animals. Sam was the captain of his group, which meant he had to keep everyone calm and organized.

Before they left, Sam felt hungry, so he packed two sandwiches and an apple into his bag. He also put a small umbrella in his bag because the sky looked like rain was coming.

At the farm, Mr. Lee was an old, honest man who did not have much money — everyone said he was poor, but he never complained. He pointed to a quiet spot near the barn. "This is where we keep peace," he said. "No shouting, no running. They get scared if it is too loud."

Sam was determined to do a good job. His group's task was to build a small birdhouse. He wanted to select the best pieces of wood, but his hands were shaky, and he knocked over a jar of nails by accident. He glanced down, worried, but Mr. Lee just laughed and helped him pick up the nails.

"Everyone makes mistakes," Mr. Lee said. "What matters is that you try again."

Sam stared at his group and felt surprised — nobody was upset with him. He worked carefully, and slowly the birdhouse came together. When it was finished, it had a small painted bird on the front, like a symbol of the farm itself.

Mr. Lee tied a red balloon to the birdhouse to celebrate. "Excellent work," he said. Sam smiled at his own reflection in a small mirror by the barn and felt comfortable and happy.

Before leaving, Sam's group made sure the birdhouse had thick walls to keep it safe from wind. They thanked Mr. Lee, who said painting was his own quiet hobby too. Sam felt proud that his group had succeeded, and he knew this day would be one to remember.

house.def: A building where a person or family lives.

captain.def: The leader of a ship or a team.

hungry.def: Feeling like you need to eat.

peace.def: A state of calm with no fighting or arguing.

symbol.def: Something that stands for or represents an idea.

balloon.def: A thin rubber bag that is filled with air and used for decoration.

look.def: To turn your eyes toward something to see it.

surprised.def: Feeling shocked because something unexpected happened.

succeed.def: To do what you were trying to do.

determined.def: Having made a firm decision to do something and not giving up.

accident.def: Something that happens by chance, often unplanned.

mirror.def: A piece of glass that shows your reflection.

thick.def: Having a large distance between one side and the other; not thin or watery.

honest.def: Telling the truth and not lying or cheating.

knock.def: To hit a door lightly to get someone's attention.

thank.def: To tell someone you are grateful.

hobby.def: An activity you enjoy doing in your free time.

pack.def: To put things into a bag.

task.def: A piece of work that needs to be done.

animal.def: A living creature, like a dog or a bird.

select.def: To choose one thing from a group.

questions.qtext: In "everyone said he was poor, but he never complained," what does poor mean?
questions.explain: B — poor means having very little money, which is why people said this about Mr. Lee even though he never complained.

questions.qtext: In "He pointed to a quiet spot near the barn," what does spot mean?
questions.explain: C — spot means a particular place, here the quiet area near the barn that Mr. Lee pointed to.

questions.qtext: In "Sam stared at his group and felt surprised — nobody was upset with him," what does surprised mean?
options: feeling shocked because something unexpected happened
questions.explain: A — surprised means feeling shocked by something unexpected, here that nobody was upset with Sam after his accident.

questions.qtext: Why did Sam knock over the jar of nails?
options: He was tired and wanted to stop working
His hands were shaky while he tried to select the best pieces of wood
Mr. Lee bumped into him by accident
He was not paying attention because he was hungry
questions.explain: B — the story says his hands were shaky as he tried to select the best wood, which caused the accident.

questions.qtext: How did Sam feel about the trip by the end of the story?
options: Upset that his group did not win a prize
Proud and happy that his group had succeeded
Worried that Mr. Lee was angry with him
Confused about what happened at the farm
questions.explain: B — the story ends with Sam feeling proud that his group had succeeded and happy about the whole day.
